_meta.name: Bandit — AI Vendor Assessment
_meta.description: Specialized assessment workflow for AI/ML vendors. Extends PB-1 scoring with EU AI Act 2024 compliance checks: risk classification, transparency obligations, human oversight, and conformity assessment requirements.
_meta.status: PLACEHOLDER — import into n8n and configure credentials before use

_setup_instructions: 1. Import this file into n8n via Workflows > Import from File
2. Configure AI provider node with your credentials
3. Set the EU_AI_ACT_MODE parameter to true for full EU AI Act checks
4. Configure Jira integration for high-risk AI system tickets (mandatory escalation)

prohibited_practices: Real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces (with exceptions)
transparency_obligations: Disclosure of AI interaction to users

nodes.name: Webhook Trigger
nodes.notes: Accepts POST with: vendor_name, policy_text, product_description, is_eu_facing (boolean)

nodes.name: EU AI Act Risk Classifier
nodes.notes: Pre-screens vendor's AI system category based on product_description before AI analysis

nodes.name: AI Model (configure provider here)
nodes.notes: Replace node type for OpenAI, Google Gemini, Mistral, or Ollama

nodes.name: Parse & Enrich Assessment
nodes.notes: Merges PB-1 JSON with EU AI Act classification result

nodes.name: High-Risk AI Gate
nodes.notes: Routes high-risk AI systems to mandatory DPO escalation path

nodes.name: Create Escalation Ticket
nodes.notes: Creates high-priority Jira ticket for DPO review. See /integrations/jira-setup.md

nodes.name: Save to Vendor Register
nodes.notes: Appends assessment to AI vendor register in Google Drive
